Class Unit

java.lang.Object
entities.Unit
Direct Known Subclasses:
Archer, Cavalry, Infantry, Pikeman
public abstract class Unit extends Object
Represents a controllable character or entity on the board. Tracks base stats (health, movement, attack) and current board position.

  • Constructor Details

    • Unit

      public Unit(int PID, int UID, String kingdom, int x, int y)

  • Method Details

    • canMoveTo

      public boolean[][] canMoveTo()
      Returns a boolean[][] (board-sized) where true means this unit can move to that tile. Uses BFS limited by currentMovement. Ally tiles are blocked; enemy tiles stop movement.

    • canAttack

      public boolean[][] canAttack()
      Returns a boolean[][] (board-sized) where true means this unit can attack that tile. Uses Manhattan distance limited by attackRange; only enemy-occupied tiles are flagged.

    • canAttackTile

      public boolean canAttackTile(int i, int j)

    • canMoveToTile

      public boolean canMoveToTile(int i, int j)

    • spendAllMovement

      public void spendAllMovement()
      Spends all remaining movement points (called after the unit moves).

    • getPlayerID

      public int getPlayerID()

    • getUnitID

      public int getUnitID()

    • getHealth

      public int getHealth()

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Returns the row (x) coordinate of the unit.

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Returns the column (y) coordinate of the unit.

    • getPos

      public int[] getPos()
      Returns the unit's current grid position as {row, col}.

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(int row, int col)

    • getSpritePath

      public String getSpritePath()
      Retrieves the dynamic path for this unit's sprite based on their owning player's kingdom. IMPORTANT: This assumes a specific file structure: /assets/kingdoms/{kingdomName}/{UNIT_TYPE}
      Returns:
      The file path string for the sprite image.

    • getImage

      public javafx.scene.image.Image getImage()

    • endTurn

      public void endTurn()
      Resets currentMovement to maxMovement at the start of the next turn.
